privileged_info_summary: The task relies on the real-world Bologna Pasubio dataset (iTETRIS project). The baseline routing configuration strictly includes exactly 8,664 'private' equivalent passenger vehicles (defined by the verifier as any vehicle in the pasubio.rou.xml file) and 112 structurally independent bus vehicles, utilizing a 111-edge network topology. The core directive to deduct a target reduction range of 15% to 25% requires excising precisely between 1,300 and 2,166 private vehicles crossing an explicit >=5 contiguous-edge cordon charging zone while fully preserving the 112 bus transit services unaffected. Finally, Cost-Benefit Analysis metrics exclusively enforce fixed rates of 15 EUR/hr for time, 1.80 EUR/L for fuel, and 100 EUR/tonne for CO2 for accurate validation.
